quiz.title: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus Quiz
quiz.subtitle: Intermediate FTC with Chain Rule (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Calculus
quiz.topic: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus - Intermediate Level

questions.question: Evaluate \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{x^2}^{x^3} \ln t \, dt\). Simplify your answer.
questions.explanation: By FTC with variable limits: \(\frac{d}{dx}[F(x^3)-F(x^2)] = \ln(x^3) \cdot 3x^2 - \ln(x^2) \cdot 2x = 3x^2(3\ln x) - 2x(2\ln x) = 9x^2\ln x - 4x\ln x = x\ln x(9x-4)\).

questions.question: Find \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{0}^{\sin x} \sqrt{1 + t^2} \, dt\).
questions.explanation: Let \(u = \sin x\), \(\frac{du}{dx} = \cos x\). By FTC: \(\sqrt{1+u^2} \cdot \frac{du}{dx} = \sqrt{1+\sin^2 x} \cdot \cos x\).

questions.question: Evaluate \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{x}^{x^2} e^{t^2} \, dt\).
questions.explanation: By FTC with variable limits: \(\frac{d}{dx}[F(x^2)-F(x)] = e^{(x^2)^2} \cdot 2x - e^{x^2} \cdot 1 = 2x e^{x^4} - e^{x^2}\).

questions.question: Determine \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{1}^{\ln x} \frac{1}{t} \, dt\).
questions.explanation: Let \(u = \ln x\), \(\frac{du}{dx} = \frac{1}{x}\). Then \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{1}^{u} \frac{1}{t} dt = \frac{1}{u} \cdot \frac{1}{x} = \frac{1}{\ln x} \cdot \frac{1}{x} = \frac{1}{x \ln x}\).

questions.question: Find \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{0}^{\tan x} \sec^2 t \, dt\).
questions.explanation: Let \(u = \tan x\), \(\frac{du}{dx} = \sec^2 x\). Then \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{0}^{u} \sec^2 t dt = \sec^2(u) \cdot \sec^2 x = \sec^2(\tan x) \cdot \sec^2 x\).

questions.question: Find \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{2x}^{3x} \frac{1}{t} \, dt\).
questions.explanation: Evaluate the integral: \(\int_{2x}^{3x} \frac{1}{t} dt = \ln(3x) - \ln(2x) = \ln(3/2)\), a constant. The derivative of a constant is 0. Alternatively, by FTC: \(\frac{1}{3x} \cdot 3 - \frac{1}{2x} \cdot 2 = \frac{1}{x} - \frac{1}{x} = 0\).

questions.question: Determine \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{x^3}^{x^2} t \sin t \, dt\). Simplify your answer.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered FTC with chain rule applications.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand FTC with variable limits. Review the ones you missed.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on applying the chain rule with upper and lower limits.
scoreMessages.0: Review FTC with variable limits and practice differentiating integrals with both limits as functions.
